title: Account Executive — SaaS Company
setting: A mid-market SaaS company selling B2B software in Australia
description: You're an Account Executive at a SaaS company selling to mid-market businesses. Your role is full-cycle: you prospect, run discovery, deliver demos, negotiate, and close. You carry a quarterly quota and earn commission on every deal you close. It's fast-paced, intellectually stimulating, and your income is directly tied to your performance.
salary: $120,000-$200,000 AUD OTE (On-Target Earnings). Base salary typically $80,000-$120,000 with $40,000-$80,000 in variable commission. Top performers regularly exceed OTE by 20-50%.

schedule.activity: Morning routine: coffee, check Slack and email for overnight responses from prospects
schedule.note: Start the day by triaging — respond to hot leads within 30 minutes

schedule.activity: Pipeline review: check CRM for today's follow-ups, update deal stages, review Clari for at-risk deals
schedule.note: Spend 15 minutes ensuring your pipeline data is accurate before the day gets busy

schedule.activity: Prospecting block: 60 minutes of focused outbound — personalised emails, LinkedIn engagement, cold calls
schedule.note: Protect this time religiously. No prospecting = no pipeline in 90 days.

schedule.activity: Discovery call with a new prospect — Head of Operations at a logistics company
schedule.note: Pre-call prep takes 10 minutes. Review their LinkedIn, company news, and your discovery questions.

schedule.activity: Post-call: update CRM, send follow-up email, fill in MEDDIC scorecard, brief your manager if it's a big deal
schedule.note: Do this immediately — don't let notes go stale

schedule.activity: Internal meeting: weekly pipeline review with your manager. Walk through your top 5 deals, discuss blockers, get coaching.
schedule.note: Come prepared with specific asks: 'I need help getting to the CFO at Account X'

schedule.activity: Demo prep: review discovery notes for your afternoon demo, tailor the slide deck, prepare for tough questions
schedule.note: Never wing a demo. 15 minutes of prep can make or break a $50K deal.

schedule.activity: Lunch break — step away from the screen
schedule.note: Protect your energy. Afternoon calls need you sharp.

schedule.activity: Product demo for a qualified opportunity — 3 stakeholders attending (VP Sales, Sales Ops, IT)
schedule.note: Tell-Show-Tell for each pain point. Address each stakeholder's concerns directly.

schedule.activity: Proposal writing: draft a business case and proposal for a deal moving to the contract stage
schedule.note: Use the buyer's own numbers from discovery. The ROI model should sell itself.

schedule.activity: Follow-up block: respond to prospect emails, send Loom videos to engaged accounts, chase stalled deals
schedule.note: A Loom video walking through the proposal gets 3x more engagement than a text email

schedule.activity: Negotiation call: final terms discussion with a prospect's procurement team
schedule.note: Know your walk-away point before the call. Trade, don't concede.

schedule.activity: Admin: update CRM with the day's activities, prepare tomorrow's calendar, send any outstanding follow-ups
schedule.note: End each day with a clean CRM and a clear plan for tomorrow

schedule.activity: Learning block (optional): review a Gong call recording, read a sales article, or listen to a podcast
schedule.note: Top performers invest 30 minutes daily in getting better. Listen to a call you lost and figure out why.

responsibilities: Carry and consistently achieve a quarterly revenue quota ($200K-$500K per quarter)
Prospect and generate your own pipeline — typically 30-50% self-sourced
Run discovery calls that qualify opportunities and uncover real business pain
Deliver tailored product demos to buying committees with multiple stakeholders
Write proposals and business cases that quantify ROI for the buyer
Negotiate contracts with procurement teams and close deals
Maintain accurate CRM data, pipeline stages, and forecast categories
Collaborate with SDRs, solutions engineers, and customer success for deal support
Provide weekly forecast updates to your sales manager
challenges: Balancing prospecting with managing existing deals — time is your scarcest resource
Handling rejection: expect a 70-80% loss rate. Most deals don't close. Resilience is essential.
Navigating complex buying committees with competing priorities and internal politics
Maintaining motivation during slow quarters when pipeline feels thin
Keeping CRM data accurate when you'd rather be selling — but managers need clean data
Managing the emotional rollercoaster: a big deal closes on Monday, another one dies on Tuesday
rewards: Uncapped earning potential — top performers earn $200K-$300K+ AUD in their first few years
Direct correlation between effort and income — you eat what you kill
Intellectual challenge: every deal is a puzzle with different stakeholders, politics, and dynamics
Rapid career progression: AE to Senior AE to Enterprise AE to Sales Manager in 3-5 years
Transferable skills: sales skills work in every industry and every economy
The rush of closing a big deal after months of work — there's nothing quite like it
careerPath: SDR/BDR (0-1 year) — learn prospecting and qualification
Account Executive (1-3 years) — full-cycle selling, carry your own quota
Senior Account Executive (3-5 years) — larger deals, strategic accounts, mentoring junior reps
Enterprise Account Executive (5-8 years) — complex, high-value deals with long cycles
Sales Manager / Team Lead (5-8 years) — manage a team of 6-10 AEs
Director of Sales (8-12 years) — own a segment or region
VP of Sales / CRO (12+ years) — lead the entire go-to-market organisation
